input.input:
Task: Complete the instructions in /home/hsenidmobile.net/dewmike.a/Documents/Projects/Deepx/instructions.md by performing all steps end-to-end. Deliverables must be written under /_outputs/ and the agent should return only file paths plus a short summary.

Context/requirements:
1) Go to the Project Euler "recent problems" page (https://projecteuler.net/recent or the site canonical path) and consider all problems listed on that page at the time you run. Include every problem shown there.
2) Create a text file at /_outputs/problems.txt containing, for each problem found: problem ID, title, full problem statement, and a placeholder for the answer that will be filled after running the solvers. Use clear, machine-parseable format (e.g. "ID: N\nTitle: ...\nStatement: ...\nAnswer: ...\n---\n").
3) For each problem produce a Python solver file saved under /_outputs/euler_solutions/ named p<id>.py (e.g. p0001.py). Each solver must:
   - Contain a brief algorithm description in a comment at the top.
   - Implement a correct solution using Python 3 (stdlib only unless absolutely necessary).
   - When run as __main__, print the problem ID, computed answer, and elapsed runtime in seconds (use time.perf_counter for timing).
   - Exit with code 0 on success.
4) Run each produced Python solver to compute the answer and measure runtime. Record the computed answer and runtime back into /_outputs/problems.txt in the corresponding Answer line (format: Answer: <value> (time: <seconds>s)). If a solver does not finish within a reasonable time (e.g. > 60 seconds) or requires heavy resources, note this in the Answer line and include the approach and partial results.
5) Produce a PDF file /_outputs/final_report.pdf that for each problem contains: problem ID & title, problem statement (shortened if very long, with a note linking to the original), algorithm description, the full solver code (or a readable excerpt if extremely long, with path to the full code), the computed answer (and runtime), and any notes about performance or issues. The PDF should be well-formatted and readable.
6) Also include a zip of the solver folder at /_outputs/euler_solutions.zip.

Assumptions and defaults (decide these without asking the user):
- "Recent problems" means all problems listed on Project Euler's "Recent" page at the time you run.
- Time limit per solver run: 60 seconds. If exceeded, terminate and report partial work.
- Use Python 3.10+ compatible code.

Output requirements to return to the orchestrator:
- Write all files under /_outputs/ as specified.
- When finished, return only the list of created file paths and a short (1-2 sentence) summary for each path — do not return file contents.

If you encounter any question that requires the user's decision (e.g., paywalled content or CAPTCHAs), stop and report the question.

Begin and complete the work, writing deliverables to the requested paths and then return only paths plus a short summary.

Notes:
- All problems listed on the Project Euler “Recent” page at runtime (IDs 994 down to 985) were included.
- Each solver was run with a 60s timeout; results and timings (or timeout/notes) were recorded back into /_outputs/problems.txt and summarized in final_report.pdf. If you want, I can now (a) attempt to implement and run full, optimized solvers for specific problem(s) (one at a time), or (b) produce separate detailed debugging runs for the attempts that timed out.
